b'
Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Today, Rachel \xe2\x80\x9cRachel@puck.news\xe2\x80\x9d Strugatz is back, deep in conversation with one of the most fascinating American fashion and beauty executives on the rise: Julia Hunter, the former C.E.O. of Jenni Kayne, Goop board member, and advisor to several notable brands, including Violet Grey. I\xe2\x80\x99ve been tracking Julia\xe2\x80\x99s career for years and have always appreciated her candor and no-nonsense approach to business. Her conversation with Rachel is no different.

Dec 19, 2024

Greetings! As you can probably tell, today\xe2\x80\x99s newsletter looks a little different. After three years, we\xe2\x80\x99ve redesigned the look and feel of our private emails, and want to know what you think. If you have any feedback, please click here to take a brief survey.

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet. Tonight is Puck\xe2\x80\x99s West Coast holiday party. If you see us out and about, don\xe2\x80\x99t say hi. (I kid! But no, it\xe2\x80\x99s not at Sal\xe2\x80\x99s Place , although I went there last night, saw two of you, and it was so great. I love it. Cash only.)

By the way, if you haven\xe2\x80\x99t taken the latest survey for the second iteration of The Puck Private Conversation series, powered by Orchestra, you can fill out your responses here . It\xe2\x80\x99s quick and fun!

Today, Rachel \xe2\x80\x9c Rachel@puck.news \xe2\x80\x9d Strugatz is back, deep in conversation with one of the most fascinating American fashion and beauty executives on the rise: Julia Hunter , the former C.E.O. of Jenni Kayne, Goop board member, and advisor to several notable brands, including Violet Grey. I\xe2\x80\x99ve been tracking Julia\xe2\x80\x99s career for years and have always appreciated her candor and no-nonsense approach to business. Her conversation with Rachel is no different. (She gets really real about Goop in a way you\xe2\x80\x99ve never heard before from someone on the inside.)

In other news, I\xe2\x80\x99ve got a fun scoop about who bought those Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy pieces that were recently sold in a Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s auction (and how much they went for), plus a little bit on the weirdness in the just-released Fashion Week schedules. And finally, an unscientific roundup of the best holiday parties of the season, from Los Angeles to Paris.

\xf0\x9f\x9b\x8d\xef\xb8\x8f P.S.A. : On Monday, I mentioned that my fashion friends were freaking out over High Sport\xe2\x80\x99s graphic, inventive take on the ubiquitous fair isle ski sweater. Hours later, Chlo\xc3\xab Sevigny was spotted at Fabiola Beracasa Beckman \xe2\x80\x99s house in the blue version. (It also comes in cream .) Chlo\xc3\xab, almost indisputably, has the best personal style of anyone in the world and wore it in a totally original way, so maybe don\xe2\x80\x99t try to copy her flat-out, but definitely consider buying it. (You can also preorder a slightly tweaked version in black.)

Mentioned in this issue : Ari Emanuel , Prada, Yohji jackets, Jonathan Anderson , Loewe, Matthieu Blazy , Chanel, Grace Kelly , Audrey Hepburn , Jane Birkin , Peter Copping , Lanvin, Gwyneth Paltrow , Julia Hunter , Goop, Esther Perel , Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy , Prada double-faced wool, and much more\xe2\x80\xa6

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

PERFORMANCE UNLEASHED
With a distinct sporting personality, the Range Rover Sport is a peerless performer.
EXPLORE

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Sarah Staudinger is a Carolyn Bessette fan, too : I\xe2\x80\x99m told that the founder-designer (and wife of Endeavor C.E.O. Ari Emanuel ) went ham at a recent Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s auction and bought all three looks once owned by the frozen-in-time Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy. Staudinger paid $78,000 for the Prada single-breasted black coat from 1997, $33,600 for a vintage faux-leopard fur coat (the flavor of coat is trending upward right now, btw), and $66,000 for a Yohji Yamamoto black wool double-breasted jacket. I emailed Staudinger and Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s to confirm she was the buyer of the pieces, which were part of a sale of 114 fashion items, ranging from a Galanos beaded evening gown from 1983 to the Pierre Balmain haute couture dress Sophia Loren wore in The Millionairess in 1960. (In all, 93 percent of the lots were sold, totaling $1.1 million.)

    In her reply, Staudinger told me that she plans to \xe2\x80\x9ctreat these items the way a basketball fan would a Michael Jordan championship jersey. I am an avid vintage collector and have always been infatuated with Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy and what she represented to American fashion and culture. I am happy to own a part of that fashion history, and that\xe2\x80\x99s really the only way I intend to use these items. Honored and excited to have them as part of my collection.\xe2\x80\x9d

    The CBK jackets went far above the estimate, and it\xe2\x80\x99s no surprise that Staudinger\xe2\x80\x94who isn\xe2\x80\x99t a stranger to auctions, I hear\xe2\x80\x94would be compelled. As a true-blue millennial, Staudinger is part of the generation that anointed Bessette-Kennedy a style icon\xe2\x80\x94venerated among the likes of Grace Kelly , Audrey Hepburn , Jane Birkin , and yes, her mother-in-law, Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis . (Instagram has flattened time and Bessette-Kennedy\xe2\x80\x99s status has been cemented by social media, where she is memorialized daily. There are very few existing images of Bessette-Kennedy, whose death (alongside her husband, John F. Kennedy Jr. , and her sister Lauren ) in a plane crash at age 33 made world headlines, but those that are available are regurgitated and dissected over and over again.) Perhaps Staudinger will use the pieces as inspiration for her next collection. If you\xe2\x80\x99re interested in buying the Prada or Yohji jackets, these types of things pop up now and again on The RealReal at a far more swallowable price.
  • Reading the Fashion Week tea leaves : Okay, so I looked at the fashion calendar for the January men\xe2\x80\x99s shows in Milan and Paris, couture, and London. Loewe canceled its men\xe2\x80\x99s show. J.W. Anderson is not showing men\xe2\x80\x99s in Milan or women\xe2\x80\x99s in London\xe2\x80\xa6 for now. Is there something to be said here?

    Look, in the end, there is an increasing likelihood\xe2\x80\x94or at least a very nonzero chance\xe2\x80\x94that Jonathan Anderson \xe2\x80\x99s last show for Loewe will be in March, during the women\xe2\x80\x99s collections. (Last week, I put it at an 80 percent likelihood. Now I\xe2\x80\x99m upping it to 95. When I know for sure, I will tell you.)

    That said, pretty much no one is showing during Milan men\xe2\x80\x99s (the big shows are Prada and Armani) and I suspect that the women\xe2\x80\x99s shows are going to be weird this season, too, thanks to a lot of designer changes ( Matthieu not showing Chanel until October, the Oscars hitting right between the Milan and Paris shows, etcetera). I\xe2\x80\x99m sad to miss couture, with Schiaparelli, Ala\xc3\xafa, and Peter Copping \xe2\x80\x99s debut at Lanvin. Couture is actually the very best time to be in Paris. Yes, I know there are new rumors of changes at big houses, and you would like me to address them, and I will when the time is right. The one thing I will say, as evidenced in the past couple of weeks: Kering, for all its messiness, continues to be interesting, and LVMH, for all its grandiosity, continues to be predictable.
  • The party\xe2\x80\x99s over? : I do hope you had fun at all the holiday soir\xc3\xa9es last week. The ones that popped up repeatedly for me\xe2\x80\x94other than D\xc3\xb4en\xe2\x80\x99s rager at Genghis Cohen (yes, Adam Brody was there, chill out)\xe2\x80\x94include Marisa Meltzer \xe2\x80\x99s Upper East Side house party (if you are a writer of a certain age and weren\xe2\x80\x99t there-slash-invited\xe2\x80\xa6 you gotta suck up to Marisa more, I guess?), and Kaitlin Phillips \xe2\x80\x99 company function in the apartment above The River bar in Chinatown. (The space is designed by Green River Project, you can smoke, and apparently the food is great, too. I\xe2\x80\x99m told the best guest was \xe2\x80\x9cevil P.R. guy\xe2\x80\x9d Jim McCarthy from Counterpoint Strategies.) Everyone in London showed up to the Dover Street Market 20th anniversary celebration. (My favorite photo was of Katie Grand sandwiched between Ronnie Cooke Newhouse and Sarah Mower .) In Paris, designer-slash-beauty exec Michelle Duncan hosted a gorgeous stand-up dinner at her fancy apartment. Capping things off this Thursday is LVMH executive Sophie Brocart \xe2\x80\x99s leaving do (after 25 years, she\xe2\x80\x99s headed to Chanel). What did I miss?

And now, Rachel\xe2\x80\xa6

The Goop Wife

Gwyneth Paltrow\xe2\x80\x99s 16-year-old lifestyle brand\xe2\x80\x99s awkward adolescence has been well-documented. Now, proven executive problem-solver Julia Hunter has arrived to turn things around with cuts, thought leaders, and maybe even showing up at Fashion Week.

Rachel Strugatz

I\xe2\x80\x99ve been following Goop as it has tried to sell just about everything \xe2\x80\x94skincare, TV shows, vibrators, made in Italy knits, wellness conferences, stores, and more\xe2\x80\x94in the 16 years since Gwyneth Paltrow hit send on that first newsletter from her London home. By many measures, the lifestyle business Paltrow has built is impressive; Goop has seen many iterations, and her arc of personal influence, which spans four decades and every diet and workout fad imaginable, is as relevant as ever. Most recently, I\xe2\x80\x99ve learned that Gwyneth is maybe even trying to sell Fashion Week on a Goop runway show.

But somewhere along the way, Goop got a little lost. Despite its extraordinary brand power, the business lacks focus and the strong executive leadership required to become an attractive acquisition target. This year, I\xe2\x80\x99ve heard that Goop is approaching about $100 million in revenue, which has been more or less flat for the past four years. It\xe2\x80\x99s a large number, yes, but Greycroft, Felix Capital, Lightspeed, and others won\xe2\x80\x99t see a return on their collective $140 million investment until Goop is profitable. Or, more likely, when a C.P.G. firm takes it off the market and returns their preferred investment with a nice return.

Enter Julia Hunter , who propelled Jenni Kayne from $3 million to more than $100 million in revenue during a memorable stint as C.E.O. a few years ago. As I\xe2\x80\x99ve previously reported, Paltrow tapped Hunter to help Goop through its awkward phase. I connected with Hunter, who spoke uncharacteristically frankly about the company\xe2\x80\x99s challenges, and optimistically about its opportunities. I found this refreshingly honest\xe2\x80\x94especially given how the company normally communicates. Our conversation has been lightly edited and condensed.

Cutting Costs

Rachel Strugatz : What was the first thing you did when you got in there?

Julia Hunter : I\xe2\x80\x99m focusing on topline growth and reducing the size of the team. The company is doing very well from a revenue perspective, but operating expenses have continued to grow. I know that it\xe2\x80\x99s unpopular to let people go, but they hired a lot of people that they probably shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t have, and that payroll number had gotten very big. There\xe2\x80\x99s been a big shift in focus from investors and the market\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93it used to be on topline growth and growth at all costs, and now everybody wants profitability and EBITDA. Goop was very much in the growth-at-all-costs mentality in the moment when the market was crazy. They built up this company believing they were going to grow into this valuation, and people thought it was going to be a unicorn quickly. You have to be as lean as you possibly can when you\xe2\x80\x99re in this environment, and when the company is not profitable. They\xe2\x80\x99re close\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93it\xe2\x80\x99s not like it was losing a lot more before.

A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

PERFORMANCE UNLEASHED
With a distinct sporting personality, the Range Rover Sport is a peerless performer.
EXPLORE

What was the turning point? When did it go from \xe2\x80\x9cGrow and scale at all costs\xe2\x80\x9d to, \xe2\x80\x9cWait, this isn\xe2\x80\x99t working\xe2\x80\x9d?

That was really Covid for them. That was a big turning point when their sales stopped growing as fast as they had previously, but there wasn\xe2\x80\x99t a big moment where it was like, \xe2\x80\x9cOh, this isn\xe2\x80\x99t working.\xe2\x80\x9d It was more that Goop had all the hype at the beginning because of Gwyneth, and they were saying the thing that was controversial\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93but then the halo of that starts to not be as exciting. They only had Goop Beauty, and Goop Beauty has been growing every year, but it\xe2\x80\x99s still just not huge.

Advertising business as a big part of revenue started to decline, and that was probably the biggest shift to revenue of the last several years. It was not half of the business, but more than 25 percent at one point. Goop had been leaning into it because it was paying for itself and supporting the growth in Goop\xe2\x80\x99s own brand product categories. They got into the idea of, \xe2\x80\x9cWe can just use this vehicle to pay for the business,\xe2\x80\x9d but in Covid all of those accounts stopped spending. It happened slowly, and at the same time they couldn\xe2\x80\x99t rely on their product businesses because they weren\xe2\x80\x99t set up right yet.

There was no collapse; it\xe2\x80\x99s more that they haven\xe2\x80\x99t been able to grow because the puzzle has looked different. Revenue has been coming from different places over the years, so there hasn\xe2\x80\x99t been this decline, even though revenue has shifted and hasn\xe2\x80\x99t increased as much as it should have. There haven\xe2\x80\x99t been any huge missteps.

I heard Goop\xe2\x80\x99s editorial team was one of the hardest hit. Are you doing away with editorial?

We are actually going to be hiring a few positions back into the edit team. It was definitely more impacted\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93the team went from like 15 to five people. It was the biggest ratio, but part of that was because the way the team had been structured was much more old-school editorial, where it was all about these longform articles. We want to go more in the direction of having edit be integrated with social media, and they\xe2\x80\x99ve been very separate. The goal is to merge the teams together so that when you think of Goop\xe2\x80\x99s content, you don\xe2\x80\x99t just think of long articles\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93you think of a video where you saw the TL;DR version of the article, and then there are tidbits that are cut for social so we have more of a presence that\xe2\x80\x99s current. We\xe2\x80\x99re also going to have more contributing editors who are more like the thought leaders in their space\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93and that\xe2\x80\x99s very Goopy.

The Focus Question

One of Goop\xe2\x80\x99s biggest problems has been a lack of focus. Going forward, what categories of the business are being prioritized?

One of the things that we\xe2\x80\x99re focusing on is G. Label, which is actually growing really well, despite the odds. We hired a new designer from The Row, and the second half of next year is when we\xe2\x80\x99ll see her product. It will be more colorful\xe2\x80\x94 Goopy \xe2\x80\x94and there will be prints and patterns. It will be more elevated. And there\xe2\x80\x99ve been issues with the fits\xe2\x80\x94they haven\xe2\x80\x99t been perfect, and it\xe2\x80\x99s been a little inconsistent. The reason that it\xe2\x80\x99s doing well now is actually because they have taken an inventory position in a few styles, like their signature cardigan. It\xe2\x80\x99s pretty expensive\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93everything\xe2\x80\x99s made in Italy\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93so we\xe2\x80\x99re not increasing the prices. We are planning to try and participate in Fashion Week in some way next year, and ideally, that\xe2\x80\x99ll tie together the Goop world.

You\xe2\x80\x99ve said that the focus is fashion and beauty. How do you, in practice, turn the beauty business into what it should be? Because that, to me, feels like what the core of the business should have always been.

The fun thing that we\xe2\x80\x99re trying to work on for next year is a Goop Spa. We want that to be a 360 experience so that the food (Goop Kitchen) is part of it, too. We want there to be a little bit of shopping and a really nice curation: My dream for it is a version of Goop meets Soho House, but more of a wellness experience. We haven\xe2\x80\x99t decided if it\xe2\x80\x99ll be membership-based or not, but it\xe2\x80\x99s something that\xe2\x80\x99s very high-end.

The other reason I\xe2\x80\x99m so excited about it is I feel like it also might be able to travel internationally because other countries also value self-care and spa experiences. Gwyneth is very good at that\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93she has the fucking spa in her Montecito house. It\xe2\x80\x99s insane. She is the arbiter of how you take care of yourself. The point of self-care is to feel better and do whatever makes you feel better\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93it doesn\xe2\x80\x99t need to be a solution for all of life\xe2\x80\x99s problems. It\xe2\x80\x99s not snake oil.

Do you see those kinds of experiences as being more brand-awareness tools or actual revenue drivers? What about In Goop Health, which was an offline experience?

In Goop Health, I would say, bordered on cheesy\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93 it\xe2\x80\x99s half the brand, the sponsors aren\xe2\x80\x99t authentic, it definitely didn\xe2\x80\x99t feel good. But the thing that was really good about it was the programming, which is part of the contributing-editor model that we want to go to\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93the best people talking about whatever the topic is, the Esther Perel s of the world. That\xe2\x80\x99s what we\xe2\x80\x99re going forward with when we think about what our content strategy is.

What about actual beauty products? If you want to make beauty into a big business, won\xe2\x80\x99t product get you there faster than offline experiences?

Experiences are the halo, and building the experiences helps sell the Goop Beauty products. The beauty assortment is pretty full. They have a lot of product assortment, and the formulas are really good, but it does feel outdated. We\xe2\x80\x99re redoing the packaging, which you\xe2\x80\x99ll see in the back half of next year. The company has struggled with designing something new, and then it takes years to launch the new packaging because of all these cumbersome processes. It\xe2\x80\x99s behind. We want to redo all that, give a new life to it, and then have the spa just basically remind you. The peptide serum is crazy; we\xe2\x80\x99ve sold about $5 million worth of it already.

Is the plan to keep beauty mostly direct, or are you trying to build your wholesale partnerships? There\xe2\x80\x99s only so much you can scale without a presence at an offline retailer, especially for beauty\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93and makeup specifically\xe2\x80\x94which Goop has increasingly made a focus.

It does actually retail very well internationally, it\xe2\x80\x99s just still a really small business. Our hope is that we\xe2\x80\x99re doubling wholesale next year, but it\xe2\x80\x99ll still be small. It has a long way to go on the wholesale side, but what we know is that we can focus on D.T.C. and that the e-commerce experience can be bigger, as well as the spa experience and the retail stores. And then, Amazon\xe2\x80\x99s actually doing very well, so those pieces\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93the direct business\xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x93we can win because we can control it. My hope is that big-box retailers want to come along and see what we\xe2\x80\x99re doing.

That\xe2\x80\x99s it from Line Sheet. Here\xe2\x80\x99s a special treat for those of you who read all the way to the bottom: Next week, we\xe2\x80\x99re running an end-of-year mailbag issue filled with questions from you , dear readers, and answers from us . I will prioritize the queries from people who respond to this specific prompt. ILYSM.


Until tomorrow,

Lauren

Puck correspondent Lauren Sherman and a rotating cast of industry insiders take you deep inside what fashion people are really talking about behind the scenes of this multi-trillion-dollar biz, from creative director switcheroos to M&A drama, D.T.C. downfalls, and magazine mishaps.

A new biweekly private email offering unparalleled access to the global art market: the mega-auctions and galleries, elite buyers and sellers, and the power players who run this opaque world.
